description: Replace the fixed training calorie-demand rule with a VO2-based zone-weighted estimate, add optional VO2max input, and expose training_kcal at the top level while permanently preserving training_carbs_g for fueling and the training meal.

userStories.title: Add optional vo2max to request and CLI contracts
userStories.description: As a user, I want to provide VO2max explicitly so the calculator can use my known fitness input instead of always predicting it.
acceptanceCriteria: Add optional vo2max field to the request contract and CLI parsing path.
Add optional --vo2max integer flag to mealplan calculate help and parsing.
Omitting --vo2max remains valid.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Validate vo2max range and request parsing
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want vo2max validation pinned so invalid input is rejected deterministically before calculation runs.
acceptanceCriteria: Reject non-integer vo2max input through the existing validation path.
Reject vo2max values outside the inclusive range 10..100.
Accept representative boundary values inside the valid range.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement kg-based VO2max source selection
userStories.description: As a user, I want the calculator to choose explicit VO2max when provided and otherwise predict it from my profile in kilograms.
acceptanceCriteria: Use explicit vo2max when it is present.
When vo2max is absent, calculate predicted VO2max as 79.9 - (0.39 * age) - (13.7 * sex) - (0.28 * weight_kg).
Map male to sex=0 and female to sex=1 for the prediction formula.
Do not convert weight to pounds anywhere in this logic.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement zone-weighted training kcal formula
userStories.description: As a user, I want training calories to scale with zone intensity and athlete profile so easy and hard sessions are estimated differently.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the fixed training calorie-demand rule with zone-specific kcal/min = weight_kg * 0.005 * zone_intensity * max(vo2max_used - 3.5, 0).
Use fixed zone coefficients z1=0.30, z2=0.50, z3=0.65, z4=0.80, z5=0.925.
Calculate total training_kcal as the sum of zone_minutes * zone_kcal_per_min across zones 1..5.
Keep full floating-point precision internally and round only emitted training_kcal fields to 2 decimal places.
Round public emitted training_kcal to 2 decimal places.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Pin training kcal edge cases and monotonicity
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want focused regression coverage for the new formula so its coefficients and basic physiological behavior do not drift silently.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover all-zero zone minutes producing training_kcal = 0.0.
Tests cover zone 1 producing non-zero training_kcal when zone 1 minutes are present and VO2max is valid.
Tests cover higher zones producing higher kcal/min than lower zones for the same athlete.
Tests cover athlete-difference cases from age, gender, or weight changes when VO2max is predicted.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Thread athlete context into the training-demand stage
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want orchestration to pass athlete profile and optional VO2max into the training-demand stage so the new formula can run through the canonical service boundary without changing the permanent fueling model.
acceptanceCriteria: Update the training-demand stage to receive age, gender, weight_kg, optional vo2max, and normalized zone minutes.
Keep the rollout scoped to replacing training_calorie_demand_kcal without redesigning the current training-meal pipeline.
Preserve current internal training-fueling behavior where still needed by meal construction as a permanent part of the model.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Rename the top-level response field to training_kcal
userStories.description: As a consumer of the output, I want the top-level training metric to be calories instead of carb grams so the response matches the new demand model.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace top-level response field training_carbs_g with training_kcal in the structured response contract.
Do not emit training_carbs_g at the top level after this change.
Pin top-level total_kcal to TDEE + training_kcal, with rounding applied only at emitted-field boundaries.
Keep existing optional training-meal behavior intact as a permanent part of the model.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Update CLI renderers and goldens for training_kcal
userStories.description: As a user, I want JSON, text, and table output to show training_kcal consistently so all interfaces reflect the same top-level contract.
acceptanceCriteria: JSON output includes training_kcal and excludes top-level training_carbs_g.
Text output renders training_kcal.
Table output renders training_kcal.
Update deterministic golden fixtures and output ordering expectations for the renamed field.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Document the new formula and phased contract behavior
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the canonical docs updated so the VO2-based formula and top-level contract change are explicit and durable.
acceptanceCriteria: Update docs/REQUIREMENTS.md with the kg-based VO2 prediction formula, zone coefficients, and training_kcal top-level contract.
Update docs/MODEL.md and docs/ARCHITECTURE.md to distinguish top-level training_kcal from any preserved internal carb-based training-meal behavior.
Update README examples and field descriptions to use training_kcal at the top level.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Clean up stale top-level terminology after rollout
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a follow-up cleanup pass so code comments, helper names, and docs distinguish top-level training_kcal from the permanent fueling model.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit internal names, comments, and helper descriptions that still imply top-level training demand is represented by training_carbs_g.
Rename or clarify stale terminology where safe without removing the permanent training_carbs_g fueling concept or changing the shipped public contract again.
Update any stale examples that still present the removed fixed kcal-per-minute rule as canonical.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
